mechanism_narrative: Sustained upward momentum in the federal funds rate increases discount rates and borrowing costs, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS change and 5-day forward returns is above -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.0%, it signals deteriorating labor demand that precedes downward earnings revisions and consumption retrenchment, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE crossings above 4.0% are followed by positive 60-day returns in >50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in industrial production and retail sales indicate a reinforcing demand-supply loop that lifts corporate revenue visibility, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint increases in INDPRO and RSAFS are followed by negative 20-day returns in >50% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, month-over-month declines in housing starts signal that interest-rate-sensitive demand is collapsing and transmitting to broad equity indices through earnings and credit channels, producing negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: In periods of elevated FEDFUNDS, HOUST declines are followed by positive 60-day returns in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month CPI acceleration exceeding 0.5% signals sticky inflation that pressures corporate real margins and forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive guidance, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL accelerations >0.5% are followed by positive 5-day returns in >55% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Positive nonfarm payrolls growth combined with a stable or falling unemployment rate confirms labor market strength without hidden slack, supporting earnings optimism and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Jointly rising PAYEMS and flat/falling UNRATE fail to produce positive 60-day returns in >60% of quarterly observations.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index signals weakening manufacturing activity and inventory destocking that reduces near-term earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines are followed by positive 5-day returns in >50% of instances over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nominal retail sales predicts positive S&P 500 returns because consumer spending strength translates directly into revenue beats for index constituents over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS increases show Rank IC < 0.03 with 20-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.
